_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning what respectful relationships look like, how to build and maintain them, and what to do when a relationship needs repairing.

_source.w: We are learning what respectful relationships look like, how to build and maintain them, and what to do when a relationship needs repairing.
y: Strong, respectful relationships are the foundation of a happy life — they help us feel safe, valued and connected.
Knowing how to repair a relationship after a disagreement is just as important as building one in the first place.
These skills help us navigate friendships, family dynamics and eventually professional relationships too.
eg: We brainstorm the characteristics of a respectful relationship — like trust, honesty, good listening and kindness — and create a class poster.
We work in groups to develop a tip sheet for rebuilding a friendship after a falling out, including being sensitive to cultural differences.
We role-play scenarios where a relationship has been damaged and practise steps for repairing it, like apologising sincerely and listening to the other person.
We compare respectful and disrespectful relationships in stories or movies and identify what makes the difference.
We write a letter to a fictional friend explaining how to make things right after a conflict.
We discuss how showing respect looks different in different cultures and settings, and why flexibility matters.
